title: The Chinese Yoke: Russia’s Return to Vassalship
author: Matthew Zalewski
summary:
In the 13th century, Muscovy, the medieval antecedent to modern Russia, was a small outpost on the periphery of Kyivan Rus. It would not emerge as a regional power until the Mongol conquest, where it became crucial in extracting taxes for tribute and maintaining the overlordship of the Khans in the region. In turn, the Mongols supported Muscovy’s rulers in a symbiotic relationship. Today, a similar dynamic is taking shape as Russian President Vladimir Putin’s regime becomes structurally dependent on China — not only to sustain its war in Ukraine, but also to maintain basic stability.
